rationale: The citing paper is a methodological design study in conjoint/multinomial choice and explicitly says it extends prior work that optimized a single homogeneous design, while the cited paper develops D-optimal multinomial choice experiment designs and implementation rules in exactly that domain. Given this strong method-level overlap (and a verified DOI match), the citation is plausibly foundational/comparative rather than a generic mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
